results.title: Meet the Biggest Threat to Nvidia in AI Chips. It's Not AMD, Intel, or Broadcom. | The Motley Fool
Nvidia Stock Slaughters Rivals AMD & Intel as Blackwell Ultra Racks 'Lead the Market by Two Generations'
Nvidia vs. Intel: Diverging Paths in Quarterly Revenue | The Motley Fool
3 AI Stocks That Can Beat Nvidia Over the Next Five Years | The Motley Fool
AI chipmakers that could steal some of Nvidia's spotlight in 2026

summary_extracted: NVDA holds ~81% AI chip market share (IDC). AMD, Broadcom, Intel chips selected by OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon. Intel Q1 blowout: $13.58B revenue vs $12.42B estimate; Intel +154% YTD but NVDA trailing revenue $188B vs Intel $53B. AMD gaining share, profit margins 14.7% in Q4. Custom silicon (TPUs, Trainium) cited as biggest emerging threat. NVDA Rubin architecture due late 2026 — yearly cadence keeps NVDA 18-24 months ahead.
